nodes.label: AI as comprehensive method generator
nodes.label_negative: AI as incomplete or imprecise method provider
nodes.grounding_span: I want AI to not only give me the various approaches, but also a step by step, clear and simple, application of the approach

nodes.label: AI precision and accuracy in calculations
nodes.grounding_span: preliminary rounding of answers leading to a compounding rounding error

nodes.label: Teacher judgment over AI suggestions
nodes.grounding_span: I will go with what I think will work best

nodes.label: AI as quick enabler with careful oversight
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI a lot because it is quick - but it does have to be carefully checked for mistakes or inconsistencies

nodes.label: AI's role in student confidence building
nodes.label_negative: AI's inability to build student confidence
nodes.grounding_span: Can AI give the student the confidence they need to succeed?

nodes.label: Pedagogical effectiveness and student success
nodes.grounding_span: without that, I may introduce a sub-optimal method for the student

nodes.label: Efficiency and time saving
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI a lot because it is quick

nodes.label: Student confidence and holistic learning
nodes.grounding_span: there is more to learning than just the content

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as quick enabler
nodes.grounding_span: it is certainly a massive enabler

nodes.label: Frustration with AI imprecision
nodes.grounding_span: preliminary rounding of answers leading to a compounding rounding error

nodes.label: Cautious optimism about future AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I will use it more and more

nodes.label: Skepticism about AI replacing human teaching for weaker students
nodes.grounding_span: weaker one's, however, probably will because there is more to learning than just the content

nodes.label: Iterative refinement through back-and-forth interaction
nodes.grounding_span: Often, there needs to be a back and forth to arrive at a desirable outcome

nodes.label: Student-centered pedagogical filtering
nodes.grounding_span: I generally know what the student already knows and what they have covered
